shared_properties: Project core palette keeps primary #D6D0C4, background #0D0D0F, and accent #E07B39.
Wall tiles keep a strict 16px column width so tilemap placement stays deterministic.
Stone breakup uses flat clusters with no sub-pixel shading.
differences: Strip size is 16x144px to match the 1x9 tile wall span defined in the GDD.
Outer edge highlight stays readable against the abyss backdrop and marks the braking contact line.
Surface chips repeat on a 16px cadence so long shafts can extend without texture drift.

context.usage: Primary collision strip for left or right wall braking during descent.

shape.silhouette: Straight vertical stone strip with chipped edge rhythm.
shape.collision_read: The contact edge keeps a clean 1px contour for braking read.

acceptance_criteria: Each 16px tile segment preserves a readable outer contour for collision contact.
The brightest highlight cluster never exceeds 4px width along the active braking edge.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds stone texture breakup, low-key lighting, and vertical shaft composition for the active environment asset.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Tilemap-backed strip or repeated SpriteBundle instances on a collision-aligned wall lane.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load the wall texture during the Loading state and reuse it through bevy_ecs_tilemap for all vertical shaft chunks.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Keep collision data separate from the sprite so friction values remain tunable in gameplay code.
Snap every tile origin to a 16px grid before building the shaft segment.
